skill.name: industrial-brutalist-ui
skill.description: Create raw mechanical web interfaces that combine Swiss industrial print, tactical telemetry layouts, strict grids, high-contrast type, and analog display effects.
skill.summary: Guides industrial brutalist UI direction with rigid grids, tactical typography, strict palettes, and analog texture effects.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown inline code, CSS examples, HTML tag examples, and design terminology. SKILL.md contains prose-only UI guidance and no executable scripts, external commands, credential access, network requests, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown inline code examples, not Ruby or shell execution. The cited lines describe CSS values, color tokens, HTML elements, ASCII decorations, and layout guidance inside SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown prose and the flagged backticks enclose examples, not executable code. No command interpreter, script file, or execution instruction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Windows SAM Database Match
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM finding is not supported by the reviewed content. The cited line contains decorative framing examples for UI text and does not mention credential stores, registry hives, system files, or password extraction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a visual style example using bracketed text. There is no filesystem path, Windows registry reference, or credential access behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections are not real crypto usage. The reviewed lines contain design language, typography guidance, or visual effect descriptions, with no cryptographic algorithms, libraries, hashing calls, or security claims.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no code or cryptographic implementation on these lines. The matches are lexical collisions inside ordinary UI design copy.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections are design-context terms, not host inspection. The skill does not instruct the agent to run commands, enumerate files, read environment data, inspect networks, or collect system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is layout, color, and typography guidance. No host reconnaissance command, API call, or data collection workflow appears in the skill.

content.value_statement: Modern interfaces can look generic when they rely on soft cards, gradients, and loose spacing. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward rigid industrial layouts with clear typography, strict palettes, and dense operational detail.
actual_capabilities: Defines two visual modes: Swiss industrial print and tactical telemetry terminal.
Specifies typography roles, scale ranges, letter spacing, line height, and font families.
Provides strict light and dark color palettes with limited accent color rules.
Guides grid-based layouts, visible compartment borders, density shifts, and square corners.
Describes halftone, dithering, scanline, and mechanical noise effects for frontend styling.
Recommends semantic HTML elements for technical interface content.
limitations: It is a design guidance skill, not a complete component library.
It does not include scripts, templates, image assets, or generated CSS files.
The visual style is intentionally strict and may not fit consumer or friendly brands.
Accessibility requirements still need separate contrast, motion, and readability checks.

use_cases.title: Design a Dense Operations Dashboard
use_cases.description: Create a high-density interface with telemetry tables, hard grid divisions, compact metadata, and clear status accents.

use_cases.title: Style a Technical Portfolio
use_cases.description: Give engineering or creative work a precise industrial identity using large type, visible structure, and restrained color.

use_cases.title: Refresh an Editorial Feature
use_cases.description: Build a declassified-document visual system for long-form content, diagrams, captions, and structured data blocks.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the Visual Mode
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for selecting a direction before design work starts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the industrial-brutalist-ui skill. Help me choose either Swiss Industrial Print or Tactical Telemetry for a web interface about [topic]. Explain the fit, palette, typography, layout density, and risks.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Screen Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for turning a product screen into a concrete art direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the industrial-brutalist-ui skill to design a [screen type] for [users]. Provide layout zones, typography scale, color tokens, component styling, and texture effects. Keep the interface practical and accessible.

prompt_templates.title: Apply the Style to Existing UI
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for adapting an existing product without changing its purpose.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the industrial-brutalist-ui skill to restyle this existing interface: [describe interface]. Keep the workflow intact while changing grid structure, type hierarchy, color, borders, status markers, and supporting details.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Refine an Implementation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for reviewing a finished or partial frontend implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the industrial-brutalist-ui skill to audit this implementation for visual consistency: [paste description or file summary]. Identify violations, weak details, accessibility concerns, and precise changes to improve the industrial brutalist system.

output_examples.input: Design a launch dashboard for a logistics command center.
output: Recommended Tactical Telemetry mode with dark substrate and one red alert accent.
Output would define a gridded command header, compact status rows, monospaced metadata, and scanline texture.
It would also note accessibility checks for dense data and animated effects.

output_examples.input: Restyle a design portfolio home page with a harsher industrial identity.
output: Recommended Swiss Industrial Print mode with off-white substrate, black structural type, and red section markers.
Output would replace soft cards with visible grid compartments and oversized typographic anchors.
It would keep case studies readable while adding registration marks, strict spacing, and square edges.

output_examples.input: Review my CSS direction for a telemetry product screen.
output: Output would flag gradients, rounded corners, mixed palette modes, and loose spacing as style violations.
It would recommend specific typography, border, density, and texture adjustments.
It would separate visual fidelity concerns from accessibility and usability concerns.

best_practices: Choose one visual mode per project and apply it consistently across every screen.
Keep accent colors rare so alerts, markers, and operational states remain meaningful.
Validate dense layouts with real content, small screens, keyboard use, and contrast checks.
anti_patterns: Do not mix light Swiss print sections with dark telemetry sections in the same interface.
Do not add rounded cards, soft shadows, gradients, or decorative blur effects.
Do not use random technical labels when they confuse navigation, status, or content meaning.

faq.question: Does this skill generate finished frontend code?
faq.answer: It provides design direction and implementation guidance. The agent can use it while writing code, but the skill itself contains no code files.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found only Markdown design guidance and false positive static matches. No executable commands or data access behavior were found.

faq.question: Which projects fit this style best?
faq.answer: It fits technical dashboards, engineering portfolios, editorial systems, simulation tools, and data-heavy interfaces that need a severe industrial tone.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill include accessibility guidance?
faq.answer: It mentions practical interface structure, but teams should still run dedicated accessibility checks for contrast, motion, focus states, and readability.

faq.question: Can I combine both visual archetypes?
faq.answer: The skill advises choosing one archetype per project. Mixing both modes weakens the visual system and can confuse users.
